items.headline: US trade court rules against president's 10% global tariffs
items.body: A federal trade court has ruled the administration’s 10% global tariffs are unlawful. This decision forces a halt to collecting the duties, impacting billions in trade.

items.headline: Zambia rejects US health deal over data and minerals clauses
items.body: Zambia rejected a US health funding deal worth up to two billion dollars. The decision halts a new US aid model over data privacy and mineral rights fears.

items.headline: National strike escalates in Bolivia amid economic crisis
items.body: A national strike by workers over fuel prices and inflation has intensified. The growing unrest creates a major challenge for the government's stability.

items.headline: Insurgents kill dozens in coordinated attacks in central Mali
items.body: Al-Qaeda affiliated insurgents killed dozens of people in two village attacks. The coordinated assault signals a worsening security crisis for the ruling junta.

sources: Russia's Defense Ministry via state media

items.headline: Russia excludes military equipment from May 9 Victory Day parade for first time in nearly two decades
items.body: Russia will not display tanks or missiles in Moscow's traditional parade. Mobile internet and text messaging will shut down across the capital on May 9.

items.headline: Iran establishes Persian Gulf Strait Authority to collect tolls from ships passing through Strait of Hormuz
items.body: Iran created a government agency requiring ships to apply for transit permission through the strait. Maritime law experts say the demands to vet or tax vessels violate international law.

items.headline: Japan fires offensive missile overseas for first time since World War II during Philippines drill
items.body: Japanese forces launched a surface-to-ship missile during joint exercises in the Philippines on May 7. China called it a significant departure from Japan's post-war pacifist stance.

items.headline: IMF warns Iran war will push 20 million Africans into food insecurity as regional growth falls
items.body: Regional growth is expected to decline by 0.3 percentage points as energy and food prices rise. The reversal comes just as sub-Saharan Africa had reached its fastest growth in a decade.
